findings.description: North Carolina state flag image is missing from the HTML rendering on the title page
findings.evidence: PDF shows the NC state flag prominently centered on page 1; HTML shows only a very small flag image in the upper left corner, significantly reduced in size and repositioned

findings.description: Title page text sizing and centering not preserved
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'HB 1743 & Why We Are Here' as a very large centered title with 'Eco-Flows Science Advisory Board' and 'Nov 8, 2010' as centered subtitles. HTML shows these as left-aligned heading text without the same visual hierarchy

findings.description: Page 2 (blank/transition slide with NC flag) is collapsed into page 1 content area in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF has a distinct page 2 with just the NC flag on a dark background; HTML merges this into the header area of page 1

findings.description: Numbered list formatting differs - HTML uses standard ordered list while PDF uses bold numbered headings with sub-bullets
findings.evidence: PDF shows '1. Improve River Basin Modeling' in large bold with dash sub-bullets; HTML preserves content but with smaller, more uniform styling

findings.description: Slide-based layout converted to continuous document flow
findings.evidence: PDF presents each topic as a distinct slide; HTML renders as a continuous scrolling document with section headings, which is acceptable for semantic conversion but loses the discrete slide boundaries

findings.description: Modeling Supports Planning diagram - nested ellipses graphic is replaced with plain text labels
findings.evidence: PDF shows a nested ellipse diagram with 'Water Supply Planning' in a green outer ellipse and 'River Basin Modeling' in an orange inner ellipse; HTML shows only the text labels without any diagram

findings.description: DWR logo on contact page is present in both but appears smaller in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows the DWR Division of Water Resources logo prominently; HTML shows it but at reduced size

findings.description: Multiple h2 headings share the same text 'SAB – What It Is', which is accurate to the PDF slides but creates a somewhat confusing heading structure. The PDF uses these as separate slide titles.
findings.evidence:
Three separate div sections all have

SAB – What It Is

, matching the PDF's three slides with the same title.

findings.description: The document has two nested <main> elements, which is semantically incorrect HTML but does not affect content fidelity.

The Modeling Supports Planning slide (page 10) loses the visual diagram context showing nested ellipses (River Basin Modeling inside Water Supply Planning). The HTML only renders two plain text lines.
findings.evidence: PDF page 10 shows a nested ellipse diagram with 'Water Supply Planning' as outer ellipse and 'River Basin Modeling' as inner. HTML renders as two separate paragraphs with no indication of the nested relationship.
